patches.find: These 9 WINs draw from the same INTERMAGNET
patches.replace:
The SAA western cell has a centre near 80°W drifting westward at 0.2–0.3°/yr (NOAA WMM 2025; Pávón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016, Earth-Science Reviews 154:96–125). The 45°W threshold sits ~35° east of the current cell centre — the cell would have to reverse direction and travel 35° east to falsify it, with no historical precedent for such a reversal on century timescales. W022 is therefore a description of current geography with a >100-year safety margin, not a falsifiable prediction. More fundamentally, the dome's field equation H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) depends only on radial distance r from the Axis Mundi and has zero azimuthal derivative — so longitude-specific SAA features (American cell ~80°W, African cell ~30°E, any specific western-boundary longitude) cannot be derived from dome geometry at all; they must be imported from WMM/CHAOS-7 output. Standard MHD does not pre-register a specific 1900-vintage SAA longitude either, but it does derive the two-cell morphology, westward drift, and current positions from first-principles core-dynamo dynamics over reversed-flux patches at the core–mantle boundary (Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PNAS 114:3543–3548); the dome adopts the end state without a derivation path.

These 9 WINs draw from the same INTERMAGNET
patches.rationale: EXP-226 tracker (WIN-040 c3): Prepend new WIN-040-specific opening paragraph engaging the 45°W threshold, drift rate, falsifiability horizon, and structural axial-symmetry argument that H(r) has zero azimuthal derivative. Resolves ISS-1402 and ISS-1403.

patches.find: The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit, and we could not locate a pre-registration of the expected value in a timestamping venue independent of the author.
patches.replace: The confirming value for WIN-040 comes from NOAA's WMM-2025 and ESA's CHAOS-7 SAA cell-position outputs, publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit, and we could not locate a pre-registration of the 45°W threshold in a timestamping venue independent of the author.
patches.rationale: EXP-226 tracker (WIN-040 c3): Replace generic post-hoc boilerplate with WIN-040-specific sentence naming WMM-2025 / CHAOS-7 SAA cell-position outputs and the 45°W threshold. Resolves ISS-1404.

patches.find: A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian, so ∇·B = 0 does not apply and flux conservation is simply inapplicable — is the defender's strongest remaining move, but it abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on. Once interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry: B(r) demotes to a parameterless surface fit to observed pole magnitudes (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1), the dome's 20% RMS "improvement" is no longer a derivation at all but a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no predictive work.
patches.replace: A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian, so ∇·B = 0 does not apply and flux conservation is simply inapplicable — is the defender's strongest remaining move, but it abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on. Once interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry: B(r) demotes to a three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1) — the dome's 20% RMS "improvement" is no longer a derivation at all but a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks.
patches.rationale: EXP-225 tracker (WIN-053 c8): Single-sentence replacement of third-horn closure sentence. (A) Change 'parameterless surface fit' to 'three-parameter surface fit — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry'. (B) Soften 'no predictive work' to 'no numerical predictive work beyond dictating two peaks'. Resolves ISS-1410 and ISS-1411.

patches.find: Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism predicts eclipse magnetic perturbations across all 39 eclipses in the ensemble. Dome adds no dome-derived equation — its FSF scaling formula is empirically calibrated per-station, not derived from disc-radius geometry; this entry duplicates the observational basis already counted as WIN-010 and WIN-025 — the ensemble is a larger aggregation of the same phenomenon.
patches.replace: Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism + Kim & Chang (2018) predict the 39-eclipse ensemble signal magnitude and geometry. The dome publishes a scaling equation (delta_Z = FSF × baseline) but derives only the station-to-station ratio (FSF = H(r)/r); the absolute magnitude borrows the 2017 BOU eclipse observation (−10.9 nT) as its fitted baseline, making it observation-anchored rather than dome-derived. Duplicates WIN-010 and WIN-025 observational basis at a larger aggregation level.
patches.rationale: WIN-068 c9 critical: finding field retains retracted 'empirically calibrated per-station' claim. FSF IS derived from H(r)/r. Replace with accurate framing: dome equation is real but observation-anchored baseline makes it non-dome-derived for absolute magnitudes.

patches.find: The dome's FSF scaling factor, by contrast, genuinely equals H(r)/r normalized to Boulder — direct recomputation reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, so the geometric derivation the dome claims is real and we retract our prior "empirical fitting" framing. But FSF is a station-to-station ratio only
patches.replace: The dome's FSF scaling factor is a legitimate geometric derivation — direct recomputation reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, so FSF = H(r)/r is real and we retract our prior "empirical fitting" framing. But FSF is a station-to-station ratio only
patches.rationale: WIN-068 c9 moderate: reframe 'by contrast' as concession-then-pivot. Lead with the legitimate geometric derivation, then pivot to why absolute magnitudes are still observation-anchored.

WIN-061 c3 critical: DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 is wrong. Correct DOI is 10.1029/1999JA900056 (Schumann resonance parameter changes during high-energy particle precipitation, JGR 104, 10111-10118, 1999). Also update the anchor text from 'storm-suppression physics' to 'storm-SR coupling' since the paper found enhancement not suppression.
WIN-061 c3: Fix second occurrence of wrong DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 -> 10.1029/1999JA900056.

patches.find: Non-discriminating: Chapman ionospheric mechanism (1933) predicts exact signal magnitude and geometry. Dome has no eclipse-magnetic coupling equation; its FSF formula uses the 2017 observation as its own baseline, making it circularly calibrated.
patches.replace: Non-discriminating: Chapman 1933 + modern Sq models (Kim & Chang 2018) predict eclipse geomagnetic signals without dome geometry. Dome has no eclipse-magnetic coupling equation; its FSF formula uses the 2017 BOU observation as its own baseline — the absolute signal magnitude is observation-anchored, not dome-derived.
patches.rationale: WIN-010 c6 minor: finding field overstates Chapman 1933 ('predicts exact signal magnitude'). Chapman 1933 was semi-quantitative. Update to cite Kim & Chang 2018 alongside Chapman 1933 for the quantitative reach.

patches.find: Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal.
patches.replace: Pass and Fail overlap on the same observable, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal.
patches.rationale: SEC-8b.3 c5 minor: 'mutually exclusive' is technically inverted — the argument is that Pass and Fail are NOT mutually exclusive (they overlap on the same signal). 'Pass and Fail overlap on the same observable' is the correct framing.
